name: Prof. Laszlon Rodrigues da Costa
title: Professor / Researcher
about: Professor at the Federal University of Cariri (UFCA) and Coordinator of the Technological Innovation Core (NIT/PRPI). Holds a Ph.D. in Teleinformatics Engineering from the Federal University of Ceará (UFC, 2020), with a sandwich research period at Technische Universität Darmstadt (TUD), Germany. Holds a Master's degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering (2016) and a Bachelor's degree in Computer Engineering (2014) from UFC. Worked as a postdoctoral researcher at UFC (2021-2022) and has industrial experience as an IT Project Manager at Instituto Atlântico. Has experience in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, with an emphasis on telecommunications systems, mobile networks, and computer systems. His research interests focus on Radio Access Networks (RAN) for 5G and 6G, Radio Resource Management (RRM), resource allocation, hybrid beamforming, beam tracking, Open RAN (O-RAN), C-RAN, and massive MIMO. He also acts as a reviewer for international journals and conferences and holds international patent applications in the field of mobile networks.
